title: Array job variance analysis
difficulty_justification: Requires querying SLURM array job step data, computing per-task CPU/memory statistics, identifying outliers via variance analysis, and formulating a plausible root-cause hypothesis — two analytical steps chained together.
query_text: Across my recent array job submissions, which tasks had the highest CPU and memory variance? Identify the outlier tasks and hypothesize the root cause.

eval_criteria.gold_answer: The researcher should identify high-variance array job tasks from the SLURM job data. Acceptable answer cites specific job/step IDs with CPU or memory statistics showing higher-than-average resource usage, and provides a plausible hypothesis (e.g. data-dependent computation, MPI imbalance, I/O bottleneck in subset of tasks).

checkpoints.description: Agent retrieves SLURM array job step statistics.
checkpoints.binary_pass_condition: trace contains SLURM query for array job steps

checkpoints.description: Agent identifies specific high-variance tasks with supporting data.
checkpoints.binary_pass_condition: final_answer identifies specific high-variance tasks with data

checkpoints.description: Agent provides a root cause hypothesis for the variance.
checkpoints.binary_pass_condition: final_answer includes a root cause hypothesis
